original_evidence: N = 4,200 adults in 12 districts; three waves (2018, 2021, 2024)
normalized_claim: The study follows 4,200 adults in 12 districts across three survey waves (2018, 2021, 2024)

allowed_language_en: follows 4,200 adults across three waves; panel survey of 4,200 adults

original_evidence: increase in green-space access was associated with higher self-reported well-being (b = 0.14 SD, 95% CI 0.05–0.23)
normalized_claim: Greater green-space access was associated with higher self-reported well-being (0.14 SD)
allowed_language_zh: 与更高的自评幸福感相关; 与幸福感存在正向关联
allowed_language_en: was associated with higher self-reported well-being; was linked to
prohibited_language: caused; improved well-being; boosts happiness; prevents; 导致; 改善了幸福感; 提升幸福感（因果义）
qualifications: Individual fixed-effects models; effect size 0.14 SD is modest
limitations: Self-reported outcome; non-random access; residential sorting; unmeasured time-varying confounders

original_evidence: The association was larger for respondents over 60
normalized_claim: The association was larger among respondents over 60
